title: Comments should not be located at the end of lines of code
description: Comments should not be located at the end of lines of codeAnalyze your codeconsistency-formattedmaintainabilityCode SmellconventionWhy is this an issue?This rule verifies that single-line comments are not located at the ends of lines of code. The main idea behind this rule is that in order to be really readable, trailing comments would have to be properly written and formatted (correct alignment, no interference with the visual structure of the code, not too long to be visible) but most often, automatic code formatters would not handle this correctly: the code would end up less readable. Comments are far better placed on the previous empty line of code, where they will always be visible and properly formatted.Noncompliant code examplea = b + c; // This is a trailing comment that could be v
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: a = b + c;   // This is a trailing comment that could be very very long
compliant:
// This very long comment is better placed before the line of code
a = b + c;

checks.message: Comments should not be located at the end of lines of code.
